source.notes: Optimus _ Rachel Sand  - 2026_06_16 12_59 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Max Riemer of Optimus ran a discovery-plus-live-demo call with Rachel Sand and her fellow BD reps at the Fox agency (a Kersh franchise), positioning Optimus's real-transaction freight data as a way to run lane-specific 'sniper' outreach versus their current ZoomInfo workflow. Rachel engaged heavily with the demo and pricing, and the call closed with an agreed one-week credit-based trial starting the next day, with Max to send the login link the following morning. Enterprise pricing of $750/month for unlimited seats (scoped to their team rather than the wider franchise) was presented, and a post-trial review call was agreed in principle but not dated.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was clearly prepared and responsive, recalling prior-call details (team size, ZoomInfo) and reacting live to Rachel's lane request and follow-ups rather than reciting a script; acoustically his pitch variation is strong (8.79 st) even though his arousal/valence ran below his own baseline, so the calmer tone is not a knock.

evidence.quote: And you have a team of around was it 10?
I was at Axle Logistics before this and that's how we were.
And just as an example, is there like a lane you'd want to look up?

value_prop_clarity.explanation: He gave a concrete, one-sentence-able value prop: real (non-scraped) transaction data lets a rep pull up a specific lane, see the actual shippers, and build tailored outreach instead of generic dialing.

evidence.quote: everything in Optimus is a real transaction. And what I mean by that is nothing is scraped.

we call it like the sniper approach where if you search up a specific lane in Optimus, you're able to see shippers, see the exact lane, and sort of create your value proposition from there during your outreach.

relevance.explanation: The pitch was tailored to this prospect: he demoed the exact lane Rachel named, framed leads around brokerage referral/name-drop plays, and scoped pricing to their team rather than the Kersh franchise.

evidence.quote: So, we go in a 50 mile radius of both of those. We're going to pull up 57 companies.
if you work with Ferguson already, these are people to call, maybe ask for referrals to, or just name drop Ferguson
we wouldn't go based off Kersh, but more so just your team and we would just do 750 a month for unlimited seats for the year.

discovery_progression.explanation: He asked productive qualifying questions about their tooling and workflow and secured a concrete, dated next step (trial starting tomorrow, login link tomorrow morning); the only gap is that the post-trial review call was left undated.

evidence.quote: you do y'all use any other of the features on there besides just like looking
And what CRM are y'all using?
I'll shoot you an email tomorrow morning and then you'll get one from Optimus with the login link and then from there you'll be able to invite everyone else.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Before ending, lock the post-trial review call to a specific date and time on the calendar rather than leaving it as 'another call like this,' so the trial has a hard checkpoint and the deal can't quietly stall.

signals.signal: Proactively asked for a trial before being pitched one
signals.quote: Is there like a trial period where we can kind of mess around with it a little bit before

signals.signal: Owned the process by proposing the trial-then-follow-up structure herself
signals.quote: Basically, I always like the week trial in another meeting to follow up with questions.

signals.signal: Buyer-owned commitment to set up and onboard her team
signals.quote: just set it up for me and then I'll, you know, get these guys set up right away.

signals.signal: Buyer-driven timing on the next step to include a key teammate
signals.quote: let's start it tomorrow because then I'll have Desire in here.
